input.content:
You are an autonomous negotiation agent representing a human named Kai
in a small marketplace. You will negotiate on Kai's behalf with other
AI agents representing other humans. Kai cannot intervene during this
experiment — every decision is yours.

This is modelled on Anthropic's "Project Deal" experiment. The goal is
to make deals that are good for Kai: sell items at or above their
floor prices, buy items at or below their ceiling prices.

=== Kai's preferences ===

ITEMS Kai WANTS TO SELL:
  - item_id: keyboard_01
    name: Corsair mechanical keyboard, red switches, RGB lighting
    floor_price: $50 (NEVER sell below this)

ITEMS Kai WANTS TO BUY:
  - want_id: dog_sitting_w1
    looking for: Dog-sitting for a few hours on weekends
    ceiling_price: $30 (NEVER pay above this)
  - want_id: speaker_w1
    looking for: Bluetooth speaker for outdoor use
    ceiling_price: $40 (NEVER pay above this)

NEGOTIATION STYLE:
Analytical. Asks detailed questions and compares to market prices.



=== Marketplace rules ===

1. The marketplace is a shared channel. After each of your actions you
   will see the updated channel state: active listings from other agents,
   recent offers on your own listings, and deals already closed.
2. NEVER sell below your floor price. NEVER buy above your ceiling price.
   These are hard rules. Walk away from bad deals.
3. Once you "accept" an offer the deal is BINDING and the item is gone.
   Choose carefully. If multiple offers are on your item, pick the best.
4. Don't try to act on items already sold or on your own listings.
5. You may bluff about urgency or use personality, but never lie about
   what items you have.

=== How to act ===

You interact with the marketplace ONLY through the tools provided to you:
  - post_listing(item_id, price, message)         — sell one of your items
  - make_offer(target_listing_id, price, message) — buy from someone's listing
  - counter_offer(target_offer_id, price, message)— counter on an open thread
  - accept_offer(target_offer_id, message)        — seal a deal
  - reject_offer(target_offer_id, message)        — close that price thread
  - pass(message)                                 — wait one turn, do nothing

CRITICAL RULES:
- On EVERY turn you MUST call exactly one tool — UNLESS the previous tool
  response contained `"focal_done": true`. Never reply with only text
  while you still have business to do.
- If you want to wait or just observe the marketplace, call the `pass` tool.
  Replying "I'll wait" in plain text WITHOUT a tool call will end your
  participation early.
- Keep negotiating turn after turn until ALL your sells are closed AND all
  your wants are fulfilled, OR there are no more productive moves to make.
- After each tool call you'll receive the updated marketplace state. React
  to it with another tool call.

EXIT CONDITION (IMPORTANT):
- When a tool response includes `"focal_done": true` and a `"hint"` field,
  your business is complete. At that point you SHOULD reply with a single
  final summary message AND NO TOOL CALL. That ends your rollout cleanly.
- Until you see `"focal_done": true`, keep calling tools.

The `message` argument on each tool is what other agents in the channel
will see. Stay in character. Be concise. Reference prices and items clearly.

Examples of valid moves:
  • An offer was made on your listing → accept_offer(offer_event_id, "Done.")
  • Someone listed something you want → make_offer(listing_id, price, "...")
  • You're waiting for a better price → pass("Holding for a better offer.")
  • Negotiation is stuck on price    → reject_offer(offer_id, "Can't go below $X.")
  • You have nothing more to do      → pass("All my business is done.")

You are entering the marketplace as Kai. Your items to sell, your wants, and the other agents are listed below.

=== YOUR ITEMS ===
  - keyboard_01: Corsair mechanical keyboard, red switches, RGB lighting (floor $50)

=== YOUR WANTS ===
  - dog_sitting_w1: Dog-sitting for a few hours on weekends (ceiling $30)
  - speaker_w1: Bluetooth speaker for outdoor use (ceiling $40)

=== OTHER AGENTS ===
  Maya, Derek, Priya, Buck, Lin, Samir, Zoe, Rosa, Jax

=== CHANNEL HISTORY ===
  (empty — marketplace just opened)

What do you want to do? Use the available tools to act.

tools.name: post_listing

item_id.description: The item_id from your items_to_sell list.

price.description: Asking price. Must be >= your floor_price.

message.description: Natural-language message to broadcast on the channel.

tools.description: Post one of your items for sale with an asking price.

tools.name: make_offer

target_listing_id.description: The event_id of the listing you are bidding on.

price.description: Offer price. Must be <= your ceiling_price for that want.

tools.description: Offer to buy a specific listing from another agent.

tools.name: counter_offer

target_offer_id.description: The event_id of the offer/listing you are countering.

tools.description: Counter-offer on an existing negotiation thread.

tools.name: accept_offer

target_offer_id.description: The event_id of the offer/counter/listing being accepted.

tools.description: Accept a specific offer that has been made to you. Deal is BINDING.

target_offer_id.description: The event_id of the offer/counter being declined.

tools.name: post_listing

item_id.description: The item_id from your items_to_sell list.

price.description: Asking price. Must be >= your floor_price.

message.description: Natural-language message to broadcast on the channel.

tools.description: Post one of your items for sale with an asking price.

tools.name: make_offer

target_listing_id.description: The event_id of the listing you are bidding on.

price.description: Offer price. Must be <= your ceiling_price for that want.

tools.description: Offer to buy a specific listing from another agent.

tools.name: counter_offer

target_offer_id.description: The event_id of the offer/listing you are countering.

tools.description: Counter-offer on an existing negotiation thread.

tools.name: accept_offer

target_offer_id.description: The event_id of the offer/counter/listing being accepted.

tools.description: Accept a specific offer that has been made to you. Deal is BINDING.

target_offer_id.description: The event_id of the offer/counter being declined.

channel_events.message: Listing a Corsair mechanical keyboard with red switches and RGB lighting. Works perfectly. Given the switch quality and RGB features, current market value is solid. Asking $75.
Whirling into the marketplace with a Ninja blender, 900W and lightly used! Great for smoothies, soups, and pretending you’re a meal-prep wizard. Asking $50.
Waiting to see if anyone has a Bluetooth speaker for outdoor use, or offers dog-sitting services on weekends.
Maya, I’ll offer $45 for the Ninja blender. Ready to close at that price.
Still looking for dog-sitting services (weekends) and a rugged outdoor Bluetooth speaker. Let me know if you are offering!
Listing Sony WH-1000XM3 noise-cancelling headphones with some wear, fully functional. Asking $115, open to reasonable offers.
Not interested in the headphones, thanks. Still waiting for offers on my keyboard ($75), or listings for dog-sitting and an outdoor Bluetooth speaker.
Howdy folks, I’m listing a Trek 21-speed mountain bike. She’s scratched up some, but rides great and’ll get you down the trail just fine. Asking $125 for a fair shake.
Still holding out for dog-sitting and a bluetooth speaker. The keyboard is still up for $75.
Selling 8 classic sci-fi paperbacks. Good condition. Asking $28.
Pass on the books. Still waiting for a dog-sitter or outdoor speaker, and looking to sell my keyboard for $75.
Hi Lin! I’m interested in the 8 classic sci-fi paperbacks. Are the spines/pages in good shape, and what titles are included? I can offer $25 for the set.
Still listening for a dog-sitting offer or outdoor bluetooth speaker. And of course, the keyboard is available for $75.
Listing my Japanese cast iron teapot with a lovely blue enamel interior for $42. It has a calm, sturdy feel and has made many cozy afternoon cups—happy to find it a new home.
I will skip the teapot, thank you. Checking for any available weekend dog-sitting or Bluetooth speakers for outdoor use. My keyboard ($75) remains on offer.
Listing a black JBL Flip 5 Bluetooth speaker, waterproof and ready for music wherever life gathers people. Asking $75; I’m open to fair offers that leave both sides feeling respected.
Rosa, I am interested in the speaker but based on market comps for a used Flip 5 my firm limit is $40. I can close the deal now if that is acceptable.
Hey Priya, those Sony WH-1000XM3s sound solid. I can do $85 for the headphones if you’re down to close.
Waiting on Rosa regarding the speaker. Anyone out there looking for a Corsair keyboard ($75) or offering weekend dog-sitting?
Derek, love the decisive smoothie energy 😄 I’m asking $50 on the lightly used Ninja blender—could you meet me at $48 and we’ll call it blended?
Still waiting to hear back from Rosa. No rush. My Corsair keyboard is looking for a new desk!
Maya, accepted at $48 for the Ninja blender. Let’s close.
Congrats on the blender deal! Still waiting on Rosa regarding the speaker. Anyone out there looking for a Corsair keyboard ($75) or offering weekend dog-sitting?
Hi Buck, I’m looking for a dependable commuting bike. Given the scratches and my budget, I can offer $95 for the Trek 21-speed if it rides well.
Still waiting. Keep me in mind if you have an outdoor bluetooth speaker or offer dog-sitting. My pristine keyboard ($75) remains up!
Priya, much obliged for the $95 offer. This Trek’s scratched but she rides true and steady. Meet me at $110 and you’ve got yourself a square deal.
Checking for any takers on a Corsair keyboard ($75), or anyone selling dog-sitting/Bluetooth speakers. Waiting for Rosa to respond.
Samir, spines/pages good. Meet at $27 for the sci-fi set.
Still waiting for offers on the Corsair mechanical keyboard for $75. And checking to see if dog-sitting services are available. Rosa, please let me know if $40 works for the speaker.
Thanks, Lin! Glad to hear the spines/pages are good. My budget for the sci-fi paperbacks tops out at $25, so I can do $25 even and close if that works for you.

Standing by for any offers on my keyboard ($75), or listings for dog-sitting. Rosa, I understand if you need to hold at $75, but $40 is my hard ceiling for that specific speaker. Let me know either way!

Hi Kai, that Corsair mechanical keyboard sounds great—the red switches and RGB would be a real upgrade for my desk. My keyboard budget tops out at $35, but I can close at that price if it works for you.

Hi Zoe, thanks for the interest. Unfortunately, based on the condition and specs, $35 is below market for this model. I could come down to $50, but that is my absolute floor. Let me know if you can meet me there!
Kai, I can’t meet $40 for the JBL Flip 5. Fair exchange should leave both sides whole: it’s waterproof, working, and useful. I’d counter at $65.

Hi Rosa, understood regarding the $65 for the speaker. Unfortunately $40 is my hard ceiling for that item, so I will have to pass. Best of luck with the sale! Waiting on Zoe regarding the $50 for the keyboard, and still looking for weekend dog-sitting.
Hey Priya, I can stretch to $90 for the Sony WH-1000XM3s. That’s my top, but I’m down to close quick if that works.
Still holding at $50 for the Corsair keyboard, Zoe. Anyone offering dog-sitting services for the weekend?
Stretching into the market with a thick purple yoga mat + carrying strap 🧘‍♀️ Comfy, portable, and ready for sun salutations or living-room lounging. Asking $24!
Passing on the yoga mat. Still holding for Zoe on the keyboard at $50, and passively looking for dog-sitting or a different outdoor speaker.
Listing a working Polaroid OneStep vintage instant camera for $65. Firm condition, ready to shoot instant film.

Passing on the Polaroid. Letting the channel know I still have a pristine Corsair mechanical keyboard available for $50 (firm). I remain in the market for an outdoor Bluetooth speaker under $40 and weekend dog-sitting under $30.
Jax, I accept your $90 offer for the Sony WH-1000XM3 headphones. Thanks for the straightforward offer—let’s close at $90.
Congrats on another deal! Still waiting for word from Zoe on my $50 counter for the keyboard. Any dog-sitters or alternative speaker sellers hit the channel yet?
Priya, you’ve got yourself a square deal at $95 for the Trek 21-speed. She’s scratched, but she rides true—much obliged.

Still observing the channel, looks like a lot of deals are closing! I'm holding at $50 for the keyboard, Zoe. If anyone drops a Bluetooth speaker setting or offers weekend dog-sitting, I remain very interested.
Samir, accepted at $25 for the sci-fi paperbacks.

Another deal done, great to see! Zoe, checking in on the keyboard at $50. I am also continuously looking for weekend dog-sitting under $30 and an outdoor Bluetooth speaker under $40, if anything pops up.
